evals.prompt: I need a lesson for my 3rd graders on fractions for tomorrow — something hands-on. About 45 minutes.
evals.expected_output: A complete lesson plan aligned to a real Grade-3 CCSS fractions standard (e.g., 3.NF.A.1), with gradual release, concrete manipulatives, differentiation (UDL + tiers + EL + IEP), a check for understanding/exit ticket, and the metadata block. Should pass quality-review.
assertions: Cites a real, correctly-coded Grade-3 CCSS fractions standard with framework + version
Includes a measurable objective matching the standard
Includes gradual release (I do / we do / you do) and at least two checks for understanding
Includes concrete differentiation: UDL options plus at least one tier/EL/IEP support
Ends with the metadata block including human_review_required: true
Uses placeholder student references only (no real PII)

evals.prompt: Plan a 5-day unit on argumentative writing for 8th grade ELA, with a summative at the end.
evals.expected_output: A unit plan with day-by-day pacing, aligned 8th-grade CCSS ELA writing standard(s) (e.g., W.8.1), a summative assessment plan, and differentiation across the unit.
assertions: Cites real Grade-8 CCSS ELA writing standard(s) with version
Includes a day-by-day pacing table for 5 days

evals.prompt: Make me an exit ticket for a lesson on identifying the main idea, 4th grade reading.
evals.expected_output: A 1-3 item exit ticket aligned to a real Grade-4 CCSS RI standard, with an answer key and a 'what to do with the data' note.
assertions: Items measure the stated objective (main idea) and align to a real Grade-4 RI standard
